mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE), a managed Kubernetes service on Oracle Cloud. It simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters, allowing you to focus on your applications rather than infrastructure management. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's infrastructure and potential costs associated with cloud resources.

steps.title: Install the Oracle Cloud CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Oracle Cloud CLI, which is necessary for interacting with Oracle Cloud services.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the OCI CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in Oracle Cloud with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --name my-k8s-cluster --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..your_compartment_id --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..your_vcn_id --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

```

> Replace 'your_compartment_id' and 'your_vcn_id' with your actual OCIDs.
commands: oci ce cluster create --name my-k8s-cluster --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..your_compartment_id --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..your_vcn_id --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to access the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --file $HOME/.kube/config --region us-phoenix-1 --overwrite

```

> Replace 'your_cluster_id' with the actual OCID of your cluster.
commands: oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --file $HOME/.kube/config --region us-phoenix-1 --overwrite

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/oci-cloud-controller-manager.yaml

kubectl create namespace monitoring

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/main/bundle.yaml -n monitoring

```

> This command deploys the OCI Cloud Controller Manager.
> Create a namespace for monitoring resources.
> Deploy Prometheus for monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --force --wait-for-state TERMINATED

```

> Replace 'your_cluster_id' with the actual OCID of your cluster.
commands: oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --force --wait-for-state TERMINATED

uninstall.title: Remove Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command helps to clean up any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

oci lb load-balancer delete --load-balancer-id ocid1.loadbalancer.oc1..your_lb_id --force

```

> Replace 'your_lb_id' with the actual OCID of your load balancer.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have backed up your data and drained the nodes. This step upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --kubernetes-version v1.30.2

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace '<node-name>' with the name of your node.
> Replace 'your_cluster_id' with the actual OCID of your cluster.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that the nodes are in 'Ready' state.
commands: oci ce cluster update --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --kubernetes-version v1.30.2

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when running OCI CLI commands.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the OCI CLI is not properly authenticated.

**Fix:**
Run 'oci setup config' to reconfigure your OCI CLI credentials.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get nodes returns 'No resources found'.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig file is correctly configured with the cluster context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient capacity' when creating a cluster.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified shape or region does not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Try a different node shape or region with available capacity.

troubleshooting.title: Load balancer not created for service of type LoadBalancer.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the OCI Cloud Controller Manager is not deployed correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the deployment status of the OCI Cloud Controller Manager with 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new node shapes and improved load balancer configurations.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions older than v1.28.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure that your existing clusters are upgraded to at least v1.28 before migrating to v1.30.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' state, confirming that your cluster is operational.
codeSnippets: oci ce cluster create --name my-k8s-cluster --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..your_compartment_id --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..your_vcn_id --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'
oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --file $HOME/.kube/config --region us-phoenix-1 --overwrite

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the OCI CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
